scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: full documented SurveySparrow API v3 surface (113 method+path endpoints crawled directly from developers.surveysparrow.com/rest-apis/<category> pages and their 113 individual per-endpoint reference pages) reviewed; 22 GET list resources implemented as streams (4 legacy-parity plus 18 new), 33 CRUD mutations across 27 named write actions implemented, remainder excluded with per-endpoint closed-vocabulary categories (see docs.md Known limits for the exclusion-family summary)

excluded.reason: the real GET /audit_logs response schema documents its records array (list/data) as a bare string[] rather than a structured object type in developers.surveysparrow.com's own OpenAPI reference (a documentation gap on SurveySparrow's side, confirmed by inspecting both the list and single-record GET pages) -- fabricating a structured schema for genuinely unconfirmed fields risks a SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS-shaped defect; audit log event subscription management is likewise unconfirmed-shape and excluded alongside it

a multi-step stateful response-building workflow (start a partial response, add answers one at a time via question_id/answer pairs, then mark complete) rather than a single-record CRUD shape -- modeling this correctly needs a compound multi-request WriteHook (Tier-2), which this connector does not have and this pass may not add a new hook package for; excluded rather than mis-modeled as plain single-record create/update

a survey SECTION is a sub-resource of a single survey's structure (grouping of questions), created/updated/deleted only in the context of one survey's question layout; no standalone list-all-sections endpoint exists to back a catalog stream, and section CRUD is bundled into survey-authoring workflows the create_survey/update_survey actions do not attempt to model in full

per-survey/per-language translation content CRUD (import/export/update translated survey text), a specialized localization workflow with its own large body-shape (translation key/value maps) distinct from the general catalog resources this connector targets

the real GET /audit_logs response schema documents its records array (list/data) as a bare string[] rather than a structured object type in developers.surveysparrow.com's own OpenAPI reference (a documentation gap on SurveySparrow's side, confirmed by inspecting both the list and single-record GET pages) -- fabricating a structured schema for genuinely unconfirmed fields risks a SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS-shaped defect; audit log event subscription management is likewise unconfirmed-shape and excluded alongside it

lists the available response-property metadata fields (schema/config describing what property keys a response MAY carry), a metadata-about-metadata endpoint rather than catalog record data itself; the responses stream already reads the actual per-response data

creates a NEW response record directly by survey_id (distinct from the /responses/new + /update + /complete multi-step flow), but its documented body (survey_id, contact_id, channel_id, variables, trigger_workflow, tags) creates only a response SHELL with no answers attached -- a real response is only meaningfully created by following up with per-question answers via the multi-step flow this bundle excludes as out_of_scope above; modeling create_response alone without the required follow-up answers would produce empty, not-genuinely-complete response records, an accepted-input behavior gap worse than not modeling it
bulk multi-record variable creation in one request (an array-of-variables body), a batch-import shape distinct from the single-record create_variable this bundle models

associates/re-associates a channel with a different survey_id (its only documented body field) -- a re-parenting action with broad blast-radius on already-collected response data's channel attribution, not modeled as update_channel this pass
